family.name: Honest Async
family.ai_native_pin: in-flight is an honest PROCESS state, not a spinner: a wait is shown not faked, what it waits on is named with a route home, and a dropped pulse is a refused lie — the honest runtime that replaces 'consume a pulse, emit a pulse, drop the second one mid-think'. Per RECONCILIATION.md (2026-06-16): runtime ('happening-but-blocked') lives on the anima/process side (tempo fast<->slow + node activation + the async edge register C11), NOT on the commitment axis — it is not a register. The one exception is a wait on the owner, which IS a commitment state: a REQUEST sitting in pencil awaiting admission (D-4).

new_patterns.name: Parked Pulse
new_patterns.intent: A unit of work that cannot proceed is shown parked — held, named, and waiting on a specific thing (a node, a gate, an owner) — never vanished into a spinner. The wait is a first-class state with an address, not the absence of one.
new_patterns.use_when: Any surface where a request hands off to slow async work (local inference, a gate, a long agent run) and the honest answer to 'where is my thing' is 'parked, on X' — not 'loading' and not silence.
new_patterns.evolution: The family's spine and the direct repair of the flagged concurrency lie ('a second pulse mid-think dropped'). Commitment is none, and that is the corrected reading (RECONCILIATION.md, 2026-06-16): a parked pulse is RUNTIME — happening-but-blocked — which is PROCESS state (anima-tempo fast<->slow + node activation + the async edge register C11), not a point on the propose->admit commitment axis. It carries no ink-vs-pencil distinction, and scoring it as one was exactly the 'in-flight is a register' error this round corrects. Its real strength is provenance, full: causality's inspector law applied to a wait — click a parked pulse and it resolves to its atom (file:line / content-hash) and to the exact admitted-real node it waits on, with that node's prompt hash. Nothing parks anonymously; the route home of a parked unit is the whole point. Agent-drivable is partial: the parked set is a pure read-only fold over the log (loop.summon), machine-readable and handed to a session ambiently via the hook, and the pulse itself is authored through a typed seed the schema validates — but the park transition is derived, not an authored gesture, so it falls short of full. Kin (round-1 nit): a unit arrives here as a parked-pulse (RUNTIME, post-submit) out of interface-ai:virtual-request-node (the REQUEST object, pre-submit, draft->valid->submitted) — the seam between the two patterns is submit.
new_patterns.non_example: NOT a spinner, a skeleton screen, or a 'processing...' toast: those say work is happening and hide where it is. A parked pulse that cannot name what it waits on is refused — an unaddressed wait is exactly the lie this pattern exists to refuse.

new_patterns.name: Visible Backpressure
new_patterns.intent: When the system is at capacity or cooling, the surface shows it — queue depth, max-in-flight, the dial in play — instead of silently dropping work or stalling behind a frozen frame. The limit is drawn, so a refused or deferred unit reads as governed, not broken.
new_patterns.use_when: Bounded-throughput surfaces — an inference plane with a concurrency cap, a loop with a per-tick step budget, any queue with a human bottleneck — where the alternative to showing the pressure is a dropped pulse or an unexplained hang.
new_patterns.evolution: Authored as the honest answer to 'consume one, emit one, drop the rest mid-think'. Commitment is none (RECONCILIATION.md): visible backpressure renders the RUNTIME field — current queue depth, in-flight count, heat/cool behaviour — which is process state (anima-tempo + activation), not a propose->admit commitment distinction. It co-renders that field against the admitted setpoint card (step_budget_per_tick, max_inflight_atoms, human_queue_cap), and that card is itself an ink record, but the pattern's content is showing live pressure honestly against the governing dial — not drawing pencil-vs-ink — so it does not score on the commitment axis. Its strength is agent-drivable, full: the field state is a machine-readable status fold (loop.orchestrate --status) and the bound is a typed, signed admitted record (--admit-setpoint, a JSON spec with --by), so a model both reads the pressure and turns the dial through a validated seam. Provenance is partial: a cap-breach or a cooled tick traces to the setpoint admission that set the bound and to the tick record that hit it, though the aggregate pressure is a fold over many records rather than one clickable element.
new_patterns.non_example: NOT a generic 'system busy, try again' banner, and NOT silently dropping the overflow: that hides both the depth and the rule. If the queue cap and the live depth are not both on the surface, capacity is being faked, and a unit shed without a reason is the refused lie.

new_patterns.name: Declared Latency
new_patterns.intent: A node that takes time declares its expected duration up front, and the surface choreographs the wait against that promise — elapsed shown beside expected, slippage made visible — so a 30-second inference is an announced arrival, not a frozen lie pretending to be instant.
new_patterns.use_when: Any node whose work is slow and variable (model inference, a fold over a large corpus, an awaited gate) where the user deserves to know roughly how long before they commit attention — kin to editorial:the-arrival, but for live runtime instead of a one-time load.
new_patterns.evolution: Authored to make a wait choreographed, not faked — the runtime sibling of The Arrival. Commitment is none (RECONCILIATION.md): elapsed-against-expected is anima-tempo / process, not the propose->admit axis. The declared estimate is a pencil guess and the receipt's real ms is ink, but the pattern's actual work is choreographing the live wait so a slow node reads as an announced arrival — it does not draw a commitment distinction, so it scores its strength elsewhere, honestly. Agent-drivable is full: expected duration is a typed schema field on the node (the baseline latency / temporal-expectation, actual-vs-expected ms, already on the causality board), so a model authoring a node declares the promise as data and the surface renders it without bespoke code. Provenance is partial: the expectation traces to the node's declared config and the actual to its latency readout/receipt, a route home for both the promise and the measurement.
new_patterns.non_example: NOT an indeterminate spinner that spins the same for 200ms and 40s, and NOT a fake progress bar that fills on a timer unrelated to the work: a declared latency with no measured actual to check it against is a guess wearing a number, refused.

new_patterns.name: Waiting on the Owner
new_patterns.intent: Work blocked on a human stamp is visibly distinct from work blocked on the system: the owner's queue is honest about exactly what needs them, why, and under which arc — so a human bottleneck reads as 'awaiting you', never as a system that has merely gone quiet.
new_patterns.use_when: Surfaces with a human-in-the-loop authority gate — an owner stamp, an arc confirmation, a realness grant — where the worst failure is flooding the human with undifferentiated items or leaving their queue silent while work waits on them.
new_patterns.evolution: Authored as the REQUEST register made concrete — and the one async pattern that genuinely scores on the commitment axis (RECONCILIATION.md's named exception: a wait on the owner is NOT mere tempo). Commitment is full: a unit blocked on the owner is a REQUEST sitting in pencil awaiting admission (D-4) — a proposed unit bound to a pen that becomes ink only when the owner stamps. That is a real point on the propose->admit lifecycle, drawn unmistakably apart from a unit blocked on the system (a RUNTIME process state, scored none on commitment in the sibling patterns) and from settled verdicts (the admitted RECORD). The distinction between 'the machine is working' and 'the machine is waiting on YOU to admit' is the whole pattern, and it lives on commitment, not on anima — burying it in tempo would be the lie this pattern refuses. Provenance is full: each owner item resolves to its atom and the arc it serves, mirrored with an arc-first briefing onto the owner's real surface and closed with the verdict's receipt id — every ask traceable to what it is asking about. Agent-drivable is partial by design and honestly so: the queue is a read-only fold a model composes and even mirrors outward (loop.node inbox, the reflector pen), but the stamp itself is owner-only (D-4) — the surface is agent-drivable, the admission is not, and pretending otherwise would be the lie.
new_patterns.non_example: NOT a generic notification badge or an undifferentiated 'pending' bucket that mixes system waits with human waits: if the owner cannot tell at a glance which items are theirs to move and what each is asking, the queue is dishonest about what needs them. Manufacturing an 'only you can do' item to fill the queue is the same lie inverted.

new_patterns.name: Cancellable In-Flight
new_patterns.intent: Any in-flight unit can be killed or retried, and that state is visible: a hard stop mid-work is safe and re-derivable, a retry is a new versioned attempt while the old one stands as history — so the operator can always intervene without corrupting the record.
new_patterns.use_when: Long autonomous runs where a unit may need to be abandoned or re-tried (a wrong-path inference, a stuck gate, a long agent loop) and the surface must make cancel/retry a safe, visible action rather than a 'force quit and hope' gamble.
new_patterns.evolution: Authored on the substrate's real mortality model, not aspiration: a hard kill mid-run is safe because a partially-written line is dropped by the fold ('it never happened') and the next pass re-derives — the property the tests actually exercise. Commitment is none (RECONCILIATION.md): cancel is RUNTIME being erased (process state), and although a retry mints a new content-hash version that restarts the pipeline (touching the lifecycle at one remove), the pattern's content is the safe, re-derivable intervention property — not a pencil-vs-ink distinction the surface draws — so it scores its strength on provenance and agent-drivable, not commitment. Agent-drivable is full: cancel is mortality (a session is disposable) and retry is a new atom version (edited bytes the schema validates, restarting the pipeline from scratch), both first-class machine gestures. Provenance is partial: the new attempt and the superseded one are both on the log and clickable, though cancellation today is the re-derive property rather than a single named 'cancel' admission — the honest gap, the family's sharpest open fork below.
new_patterns.non_example: NOT a cancel button that leaves a half-written record behind, and NOT a retry that silently overwrites the prior attempt: if a kill can corrupt the log or a retry can erase its own history, the intervention is unsafe and the pattern is faked. History is never retro-invalidated — superseded, never erased.

open_questions: RESOLVED (RECONCILIATION.md, 2026-06-16) — the round-1 'is in-flight/parked a fifth register the rubric cannot see' fork is closed. Parked/in-flight is NOT a register: it is RUNTIME process state, carried by anima-tempo (fast<->slow) + node activation + the async edge register C11. The commitment axis stays the propose->admit binary (ink = admitted, pencil = proposed/simulated). Accordingly the four runtime patterns here re-score commitment=none; only waiting-on-owner scores on commitment, as a REQUEST in pencil awaiting the owner's admission (D-4). The async 'fifth register' issue is addressed by this re-score, not by admitting a new register.

waiting-on-owner scores commitment=full + provenance=full but agent_drivable=partial — and parked-pulse's park transition is likewise derived, not agent-authored — and in both cases the partial is honest, not a gap to close: the owner stamp (D-4) and the derived park transition are deliberately NOT agent-driven. This raises whether 'agent-drivable' should reward a surface that correctly REFUSES to let an agent drive a human or derived decision, rather than scoring it down for it — a virtue of 'honestly un-drivable', not a deduction. Tied to RECONCILIATION's proposed C19 RegisterFacet, which awaits bdo's bless (D-4, his spec).

cancellable-inflight grounds 'cancel' in the substrate's mortality + torn-tail re-derive rather than a first-class 'cancel' admission record. That is real and tested, but it means cancel is a property of the runtime, not an authored gesture with its own receipt — so the visible 'cancelled' state is inferred, not stamped. Whether honest async needs an explicit cancel admission (so the act of abandoning carries its own provenance) is the family's sharpest open fork.
